auth.signInSubtitle: Sign in with your GitHub account to continue.
auth.footerTagline: Manage assignments and submissions via GitHub.
auth.deviceStep2Hint: Paste the code there, authorize the app, then come back here.
auth.deviceStep3Hint: Once you authorize on github.com, this page should detect it within a few seconds.
auth.revokedBody_prefix: This app's access was revoked or the session timed out — requests are failing with
auth.missingScopesBody_suffix_one: . Some actions may fail until it is granted.
auth.missingScopesBody_suffix_other: . Some actions may fail until they are granted.
auth.reauthorizeHint: If re-authorizing doesn't clear this, an organization owner may need to approve the app in the org's OAuth policy settings.

students.addHint: Enter a GitHub username, an email, or both. Students complete their details when they enroll.
students.unenrollBodySuffix: {{classroom}} classroom. Student assignment repositories will not be deleted.
students.unenrollPendingNote: Their pending organization invite will be cancelled.
students.unenrollOnboardingNote: Their enrollment will be reset (their onboarding repository is removed), so a fresh invite starts over.
students.unenrollSelfPrefix: This is your signed-in account, so it will stay in the
students.unenrollSelfSuffix: organization. Remove yourself from the organization's people page if you really intend to.
students.unenrollMemberPrefix: This only unenrolls them from this classroom — their
students.unenrollMemberSuffix: organization membership is kept (they may be in your other classes). To remove someone from the organization, use the Members page in organization settings.
students.nativeInviteHint: Advanced: share this so students can accept the org invite directly on GitHub. Most students use the onboarding link above instead.
students.onboardingLinkHint: Email this onboarding link to students you invited by email:
students.matchAccountTitle: Joined the org without onboarding — match their GitHub account
students.matchBodyPrefix: was invited by email and appears to have joined the
students.matchBodySuffix: organization without onboarding, so we can't tell which GitHub account is theirs. Pick the account that belongs to this student to complete their enrollment.
students.matchNoCandidates: No unmatched organization members to choose from. If this student hasn't accepted their invite yet, wait for them to join. To remove an unidentifiable member, use the Members page in organization settings.
students.matchSelectHint: Select an account above to enable matching.
students.resendMissingId: Can't re-send the invite for {{username}}: missing GitHub id. Re-add them to the roster.
students.resendAllRateLimited: Re-sent {{resent}} before GitHub rate-limited the request; {{failed}} failed{{failedList}}{{remaining}}. Wait a bit and try again.
students.markEnrolledTitle: Already an organization member — mark enrolled
students.readySubtitle_one: {{count}} student has onboarded. Confirm to add them to your roster.
students.readySubtitle_other: {{count}} students have onboarded. Confirm to add them to your roster.
students.inviteStatusOwnerOnly: Invite status requires organization owner access, so it isn't shown here.
students.reportsErrored: Couldn't check who has onboarded (the organization repositories couldn't be read). The "Ready for enrollment confirmation" list may be incomplete — refresh to retry.
students.awaitingSubtitle: Invited, but haven't completed onboarding yet.
students.resendAllBodyPrefix: All pending organization invitations will be
students.resendAllBodyMiddle: . After this, every student who is not already a member of
students.resendAllBodySuffix: will receive a new invitation email.
students.editingSuffix: . Their GitHub identity can't be changed here.
students.emailHelpReportedLocked: This is the email the student reported when onboarding. It's part of the identity that enrollment confirms, so it can't be changed until you confirm enrollment.
students.emailHelpNotEnrolled: This student isn't enrolled yet. Their email is part of the identity that onboarding confirms, so it can't be changed until enrollment is confirmed.
students.emailHelpNoIdentity: This student has no GitHub identity yet, so their email is their only identifier and can't be changed here. Unenroll and re-add them to change it.
students.emailHelpEnrolledChange: This student is already enrolled. Changing their email won't re-bind their confirmed GitHub identity; it only affects future email-based matching.
students.uploadRosterHint: Upload a CSV or text file with one GitHub username per line.
students.noValidUsernames: No valid GitHub usernames were found in this file.
students.keepTabOpen: Keep this tab open while the import is running. Students are being validated and added to the classroom.
students.couldNotAddToTeam: Could not add this user to the team

language.label: Language
language.shareHint: Copy a link that opens the site in a specific language — handy for sharing with students who speak it (e.g. Spanish).
language.browseHint: Install a ready-made translation. These are generated from the base language and reviewed before publishing.
language.browseEmpty: No additional languages are available right now.
language.installTitle: Install from a file or URL
language.installHint: Install a language pack: translate the base en.json and load it here. The language code is detected from the file name; enter it below only if that fails.
language.errorCodeFirst: Enter a language code (e.g. de, fr, ja) first.
language.errorCodeUndetectable: Couldn't detect the language code from the file — enter it below and try again.
language.errorUrlRequired: Enter a URL to fetch the pack from.
language.errorRegistry: Couldn't load the available languages. Try again.

validation.githubOrEmailRequired: Enter a GitHub username or an email.
validation.assignmentSlugTaken: An assignment "{{slug}}" already exists in this classroom.
validation.groupSizeRange: Group size must be a whole number between {{min}} and {{max}}.

toasts.classroomSaveConflict: Couldn't save — another change landed first. Please try again.
toasts.assignmentCreated: Assignment created. It may take a moment to appear.
toasts.classroomCreated: Classroom created. It may take a moment to appear.

orgs.emptyBody: Organizations you belong to that use Classroom 50 will appear here. If you expect one, ask your instructor to confirm you've been added, then refresh.

missingNotice.title: Not seeing your organization?
missingNotice.body: Classroom 50 can only show GitHub organizations that you have explicitly granted access to during sign-in. If an organization is missing, you may need to update your OAuth permissions.

card.planTitleFree: Free plan — Classroom 50 needs a Team or Enterprise organization
card.planTitlePaid: GitHub plan — Classroom 50 can be set up on Team and Enterprise plans

assignments.archivedNotice_prefix: This classroom is archived — new assignments and student accepts are disabled. Unarchive it from

form.name: Assignment Name
form.slugHelp: Used in the assignment's repository names. Auto-filled from the name; edit it if you like. Must be unique in this classroom.
form.maxGroupSizeTitle: Must be a whole number between {{min}} and {{max}}
form.feedbackPrHelp: Open a pull request per repo for inline review of each submission.
form.advancedHelp: Optional runtime overrides. Leave blank for the defaults (ubuntu-latest + Python 3.12).
form.dockerImageHelp: Run the autograder inside this public image.
form.containerUserHelp_suffix: if checkout fails with a permission error.
form.setupCommandHelp: Runs once before grading (e.g. to compile). Added as a leading 0-point autograding step named “setup”.
form.allowedFilesHelp: Ordered {{gitignore}}-style patterns, one per line, defining which files belong to a submission (last match wins, {{bang}} re-includes). E.g. {{star}} then {{example}} allows only {{result}}. Leave blank to allow every file.
form.passThresholdHelp: Off by default. When on, the gradebook marks submissions passing/failing against the bar below (Passing rollup, score badges, passing/failing filter). A display threshold only — it does not change a student's actual score.

runner.label: GitHub Runner
runner.selfHostedHint_prefix: Combine comma-separated labels for a self-hosted runner (e.g.
runner.noRunnerMatch: No runner matches {{labels}} — check the spelling.

validation.maxGroupSizeInvalid: Max group size must be a valid number.
validation.passThresholdRange: Pass threshold must be a whole number between {{min}} and {{max}}.

autograder.heading: Autograding Tests
autograder.empty: No autograding tests have been defined yet.
autograder.editTestHint: Pick a test type, then fill in the command and pass criteria. Commands run in the student's repository checkout.
autograder.inputPlaceholder: optional — text fed to the command on stdin
autograder.comparisonIncluded: Included — expected appears anywhere in the output
autograder.comparisonExact: Exact — output equals expected (surrounding whitespace ignored)
autograder.exitCodeHint: Leave empty to require a successful exit (0).
autograder.pythonNote_prefix: The run command should invoke pytest (e.g.
autograder.pythonNote_suffix: ) against test files in the assignment template. Points are split across the cases pytest discovers.

io.label: Input/Output
io.hint: Run a command, feed it stdin, and compare its stdout against an expected value.

run.label: Run command
run.hint: Run a command and pass when its exit code matches (0 by default).

python.label: Python (pytest)
python.hint: Run a pytest command; points are split across discovered test cases at grade time.

table.caption: Assignments
table.deleteDescription_3: classroom. Student assignment repositories will not be deleted.

template.label: Template Repository
template.help: Optional. Students receive a copy of this repository. Leave blank for an empty repo with just the autograder.
template.privateHasAccess_2: ). The classroom team already has read access — students can copy it.
template.privateWillGrant_2: ). The classroom team doesn't have access yet; it'll be added automatically when you create the assignment, so students can copy it.
template.okVerify_2: ). If {{owner}} restricts third-party apps, students can't copy it until an owner approves Classroom 50.
template.notVisible: {{owner}}/{{repo}} isn't visible to you. Make it public or copy it into {{org}}.
template.notTemplate: {{owner}}/{{repo}} isn't a template repo. Enable it in the repo's Settings.
template.restricted: {{owner}} blocked access to {{repo}}. It restricts third-party apps; an owner must approve Classroom 50.
template.unknown: Couldn't verify {{owner}}/{{repo}} now. It's rechecked when students accept.
template.privateOutOfOrg: {{owner}}/{{repo}} is private and outside {{org}}. Make it public or copy it into {{org}}.
template.noBranch_1: {{owner}}/{{repo}} has no default branch. Push a commit, or add
template.rateLimited: GitHub rate limit hit. Try again shortly.

published.unlistedWarning: This classroom uses an unlisted URL: its resources are served at an unguessable path instead of a guessable one. This is obscurity, not access control — the link is the only thing guarding the data, anyone who has it can read the file, and links can leak (browser history, referrers, search crawlers). Share the per-assignment accept link or CLI command (which include the key) from each assignment's page.

banner.title: Everything below is served publicly over the internet
banner.body: Your classroom50 config repo is private, but its GitHub Pages site is public by design — students and the autograder fetch these files without authenticating. Only the allow-listed files below are published; anything else in the repo (your roster, internal classroom fields, service token) stays private. A classroom can use an unlisted URL to make its files harder to find, but that is obscurity, not access control — the files are still public to anyone who has the link.

published.perClassroomDescription: Assignment manifests and autograders for each classroom.
published.noClassrooms: No classrooms yet. Once you create one and it publishes, its resources will appear here.

classroomsIndex.label: Classrooms index
classroomsIndex.description: Public list of this org's classrooms with allow-listed fields only (name, term, short name). Internal data such as the GitHub team is deliberately stripped. Lets students confirm this is a real Classroom 50 org.

runner.label: Autograde runner engine
runner.description: The generic grading bootstrap fetched by every student repo's autograde workflow. Org-agnostic — identical for every organization, contains no classroom data.

feedbackPr.label: Feedback PR helper
feedbackPr.description: Generic helper that opens/refreshes the per-submission Feedback PR. Org-agnostic — contains no classroom data.

assignmentsManifest.label: Assignments manifest
assignmentsManifest.description: Every assignment's slug, mode, template, autograder, runtime, declarative tests, and allowed-files list for this classroom. Read by students (accept/submit) and the autograde runner.

classroomAutograder.label: Classroom default autograder
classroomAutograder.description: The fallback grading script used when an assignment has no per-assignment autograder. Only present if you ran autograder set-default.

autograderBundle.label: Autograder bundle — {{name}}
autograderBundle.description: Per-assignment grading bundle (autograder.py + fixtures + materialized tests) downloaded by the autograde runner at grade time.

autograderShim.label: Autograder workflow shim — {{name}}
autograderShim.description: Non-default autograder workflow shim fetched when a student accepts an assignment that overrides the default runner.

notInvited.title: Nothing to do here yet
notInvited.body_prefix: We couldn't find an invitation for your account to the
notInvited.waitingBody_prefix: If your instructor has invited you to
notInvited.waitingBody_suffix: , check your email for the GitHub invitation and accept it, then return to this page and refresh. Otherwise, there's nothing you need to do here right now.

pending.title: Pending confirmation
pending.message: Your details are in — your instructor just needs to confirm your enrollment. There's nothing more for you to do here.
pending.takingBack: Taking you back to your assignment…
pending.enrolledManual: You're enrolled. If you're not redirected automatically, continue to your assignment below.

allSet.title: You're all set
allSet.message: You already have access to this classroom — there's nothing more you need to do here. You can accept assignments your instructor shares with you.

form.title: Confirm your enrollment
form.titleWithReturn: Confirm your enrollment before accepting the assignment
form.subtitle_prefix: This links your GitHub account to your instructor's class roster for
form.emailHint: Enter the email your instructor used to invite you, so they can match you to the class roster.
form.genericError: Something went wrong. Please try again.

setup.pageSubheading: This will set up your GitHub organization to use Classroom 50.
setup.setupComplete: Organization setup is complete. Review the steps below, then continue to set the service token.
setup.allSetBody: Your organization is ready to use Classroom 50. Head to your organization to create your first classroom and assignments.
setup.notAdmin: Classroom 50 setup requires org owner permissions. Ask an org owner to run setup.
setup.notTeamOrEnterprise: GitHub Pages from a private repository may require GitHub Team or Enterprise Cloud. You can continue setup, but published assignments may not be accessible until Pages is available for this org.

assignmentSettings.heading: Assignment Settings
assignmentSettings.editSuccess: Your assignment has been edited successfully!
assignmentSettings.archivedNotice_prefix: This classroom is archived — its assignments are read-only. Unarchive it in
assignmentSettings.notAccepted_prefix: You do not have this assignment yet! Do you need to
assignmentSettings.individual_prefix: This is an individual assignment — there are no group settings to manage. View your
assignmentSettings.collaboratorsHint_prefix: Add or remove collaborators for this assignment repository. This assignment allows up to
assignmentSettings.collaboratorsHint_suffix_one: student in addition to the group owner.
assignmentSettings.collaboratorsHint_suffix_other: students in addition to the group owner.

serviceToken.title: Service Token
serviceToken.info_1: Classroom 50 needs a service token, a fine-grained Personal Access Token (PAT) with read and write access to the repositories in your classroom’s GitHub organization. It is stored as the
serviceToken.info_3: config repo, where the score-collection workflow uses it to read student submissions and the regrade workflow uses it to re-run student autograde workflows.
serviceToken.statusPresent: A service token is already set
serviceToken.replaceNote: Saving below will replace the existing token (an update). The old token is overwritten in place.
serviceToken.patDescription: Service token for Classroom 50 score collection and regrading. Contents: Read and write + Actions: Read and write on all repositories in the {{org}} organization
serviceToken.generateHint_prefix: Opens GitHub’s token form with the name, resource owner, expiry, and
serviceToken.generateHint_permissions: Contents: Read and write + Actions: Read and write
serviceToken.beforeGenerating: On the GitHub page, before generating the token:
serviceToken.repoAccess_warning: “Only select repositories” will miss student repos created later and break score collection.
serviceToken.permissions_warning: Read collects scores; write lets the regrade workflow re-run student autograde workflows.
serviceToken.rotateNote: The token expires after the period you set above. When it expires, the score-collection and regrade workflows will fail until you generate a new token and save it here again, so set a reminder to rotate it before then.
serviceToken.pasteBelow: After generating the token on GitHub, copy it and paste it below.
serviceToken.validateHint_prefix: We’ll check the token is valid before saving. Double-check you chose
serviceToken.saveError: Could not validate or save the token.

audit.title: Organization policy
audit.planBadgeTitle: GitHub plan — determines which policies are in scope (Enterprise unlocks additional member-privilege fields)
audit.description: What Classroom 50 configures on your behalf, and whether anything has drifted from the expected lockdown.
audit.auditError: Couldn't run the policy audit. Try re-checking.
audit.fixError: Couldn't apply the fix. You may lack owner permissions, or an enterprise policy blocks this change.
audit.readError: Couldn't read the organization to audit the lockdown. Check your access and retry.
audit.driftCanFix: Use “Fix it” on a drifted setting, or re-run setup below to re-apply everything at once.
audit.driftCannotFix: Ask an organization owner to repair the drift — these changes require owner permissions.
audit.confirmByHandBody: GitHub exposes no API to read these settings, so we can't verify them automatically — confirm each one on the member privileges page.
audit.memberPermsHint: The full list of organization member privileges Classroom 50 sets, including the ones already in place. Drifted ones are called out above.

rerun.title: Re-run setup
rerun.description: Re-apply every Classroom 50 organization setting. Safe to run any time — it only changes settings that have drifted.
rerun.requiresOwnerNote: Re-running setup requires organization owner permissions. Ask an org owner to run it.
rerun.failed: Re-run setup did not complete — a required step failed. Review the steps above, resolve the issue, and run it again.
rerun.finishedWithWarnings_one: Setup finished, but 1 step needs attention. See the message on each flagged step above — some settings may be controlled by an organization or enterprise policy and have to be set on GitHub.
rerun.finishedWithWarnings_other: Setup finished, but {{count}} steps need attention. See the message on each flagged step above — some settings may be controlled by an organization or enterprise policy and have to be set on GitHub.
rerun.success: Setup re-applied successfully — every organization setting is in place.

teardown.title: Danger zone
teardown.description_prefix: Tear down this organization by deleting
teardown.description_suffix: config repo, and removing the GitHub team of every classroom. This is irreversible.
teardown.confirmTitle: Delete every repository in this org?
teardown.executeError: Teardown failed. Some repositories may not have been deleted.

preflight.title: Organization preflight check failed
preflight.body_one: An issue was found with this organization's {{categories}}.
preflight.body_other: Issues were found with this organization's {{categories}}.

overwrite.title: Update workflow files to the latest version?
overwrite.body_one: 1 Classroom 50 workflow/script file in your config repo differs from the latest bundled version and will be overwritten:
overwrite.body_other: {{count}} Classroom 50 workflow/script files in your config repo differ from the latest bundled version and will be overwritten:
overwrite.warning_prefix: If you customized any of these files, overwriting resets your changes to the bundled version — choose
overwrite.warning_suffix: to leave them untouched and continue with everything else.

orgDefaults.title: Organization safety defaults
orgDefaults.what: Locks down organization member privileges to safe defaults (base permissions, repo creation, visibility changes, and related settings).
orgDefaults.why: Stops students from changing or deleting each other's repositories, or making private classroom work public.
orgDefaults.remediation: Open the org member-privileges page and apply the flagged settings by hand — some are controlled by an enterprise policy and can only be set there.

orgActions.title: Actions permissions
orgActions.what: Enables GitHub Actions for the organization.
orgActions.why: Autograding and the published assignment site run as Actions workflows, so they can't run until Actions is enabled.
orgActions.remediation: Open the org Actions settings and allow Actions to run (set the policy to allow all actions, or at minimum the workflows Classroom 50 ships).

orgPrCreation.title: Actions pull request creation
orgPrCreation.what: Allows GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests in the organization.
orgPrCreation.why: Some Classroom 50 workflows open pull requests on a student's behalf; without this they fail with a permissions error.
orgPrCreation.remediation: Open the org Actions settings and enable "Allow GitHub Actions to create and approve pull requests".

configRepo.title: Config repository
configRepo.what: Creates the private classroom50 configuration repository in the organization.
configRepo.why: Classroom 50 has no backend — this repo holds all classroom config, manifests, workflows, and scores.
configRepo.remediation: This is a required step. Re-run setup; if it keeps failing, confirm you have owner permissions and that an org/enterprise policy isn't blocking private repository creation.

skeleton.title: Skeleton files
skeleton.what: Commits or updates the workflow and script files Classroom 50 needs in the classroom50 repository.
skeleton.why: These bundled files run autograding, publishing, and scoring; re-running setup also upgrades them to the latest version.
skeleton.remediation: This is a required step. Re-run setup; if it keeps failing, check that you can push to the classroom50 repo's default branch.

branchProtection.title: Branch protection
branchProtection.what: Protects the classroom50 repository's main branch.
branchProtection.why: Keeps the source-of-truth config and scores from being force-pushed or deleted.
branchProtection.remediation: Open the repository branch settings and protect the main branch, or re-run setup to re-apply it.

workflowPermissions.title: Workflow permissions
workflowPermissions.what: Sets the default GITHUB_TOKEN workflow permissions for the classroom50 repository.
workflowPermissions.why: Autograding and publishing workflows need write access to commit results and deploy Pages.
workflowPermissions.remediation: Open the repository Actions settings and set workflow permissions to read and write, or re-run setup.

reusableWorkflowAccess.title: Reusable workflow access
reusableWorkflowAccess.what: Allows other repositories in the org to use the reusable workflows in classroom50.
reusableWorkflowAccess.why: Assignment repos call the shared autograding workflow from classroom50; without access sharing, their runs fail.
reusableWorkflowAccess.remediation: Open the repository Actions settings and, under "Access", allow access from repositories in the organization. Or re-run setup.

pages.title: GitHub Pages
pages.what: Enables GitHub Pages for the classroom50 repository (built from Actions).
pages.why: Published assignment instructions and dashboards are served from this Pages site.
pages.remediation: Open the repository Pages settings and enable Pages with the GitHub Actions source. Pages from a private repo may require GitHub Team or Enterprise.

rulesets.title: Branch protection rulesets
rulesets.what: Creates organization rulesets that protect classroom and assignment repositories.
rulesets.why: Rulesets enforce the classroom guardrails (protected branches, restricted deletions) across every repo the org creates.
rulesets.remediation: Open the org rulesets page to review them, or re-run setup to re-apply. Rulesets may require GitHub Team or Enterprise.

skeletonDrift.title: Classroom workflow files are out of date
skeletonDrift.body: The GitHub Actions workflows in this org's classroom50 config repo are out of date. Re-run onboarding to update them and keep grading, score collection, and Pages publishing working.
skeletonDrift.overwriteWarning: Updating overwrites the scaffolded workflow files (collect-scores, regrade, publish-pages, autograde-runner) with the defaults, replacing any manual edits — you'll confirm each one first.

classes.configRepoTitle: Open this classroom's folder in the classroom50 config repo on GitHub
classes.archivedReadOnlyNotice: This classroom is archived — settings are read-only. Use Unarchive above to make changes.
classes.deleteClassroomBody_3: organization. Student assignment repositories will not be deleted.
classes.archiveBody_suffix: : it drops out of the default classes list and stops accepting new assignments and students. Its roster and assignments are kept, and you can unarchive it later.
classes.unarchiveBody_suffix: to active: it returns to the default classes list and can accept new assignments and students again.

empty.title: No classrooms yet
empty.body: Create your first classroom to start adding assignments, importing students, and managing submissions.

join.title: Join this classroom
join.body_prefix: You have a pending invitation to join
join.body_suffix: . Accept the invitation to access your classroom assignments.
join.error: Unable to join the organization. Please try again.

repo.noDescription: No description provided for this assignment repository.
repo.emptyBody: Repositories you can maintain will appear here once assignments have been created for this organization.

form.name: Classroom Name
form.protectPagesLabel: Use an unlisted link for this classroom
form.protectPagesHint: Publishes this classroom's assignment data at an unguessable URL instead of one anyone can reach by guessing the org name. This is obscurity, not real access control: anyone who gets the link can read it, and links can leak (browser history, referrers, search crawlers). Off by default.
form.accessKeyHelp: {{description}}. Accept the generated key or type your own. It becomes part of every published URL for this classroom, so treat it like a shared password — anyone who has the link can read the data. It can't be changed later without re-accepting assignments.
form.onboardingCleanupHint: What to do with a student's onboarding repository once their GitHub identity is reconciled into the roster.
form.onboardingCleanupDelete: Delete (default; removes the repo after reconcile)

staff.heading: Staff & roles
staff.githubTeamsTitle: Open this organization's teams on GitHub
staff.description: Instructors and TAs get write access to this organization's Classroom 50 configuration. TAs see the same classroom content as instructors, but organization and classroom settings stay instructor-only.

accept.alreadyAcceptedHeading: You've already accepted this assignment. Open your repository to keep working on it.
accept.acceptHeading: Accept this assignment to get your own copy of the starter code repository.
accept.pastDueWarning: This assignment is past due. You can still accept it, but check with your instructor about late submissions.
accept.errorGeneric: Something went wrong while accepting the assignment.
accept.errorRetryHint: This is safe to retry — address anything noted above if you can, then use the button below. Some errors (rate limits, GitHub hiccups) just need a moment before retrying.

notFound.title: Assignment not found
notFound.body_prefix: We couldn't find an assignment matching
notFound.body_suffix: in this classroom. The link may be incorrect, or the assignment may not have been published yet.
notFound.checkUrl_prefix: Check that the URL is correct, or ask your instructor to confirm that this assignment has been added to

notOrgMember.title: Not an org member
notOrgMember.body_prefix: You are not currently a member of the
notOrgMember.inviteBody_prefix: Your instructor needs to invite you to the
notOrgMember.inviteBody_suffix: class roster before you can accept this assignment.
notOrgMember.afterAccepting: After accepting the GitHub organization invite, return to this page and try again.

ssoRequired.title: Authorize single sign-on
ssoRequired.body_suffix: organization requires SAML single sign-on (SSO), and your current session isn't authorized for it yet. You may already be a member — you just need to authorize SSO before continuing.
ssoRequired.instructions: Open this assignment link from within your school's learning management system (LMS) so you're signed in through single sign-on first, or use the button below to authorize single sign-on for this organization, then reload this page.

repair.hint: Autograding not running, or setup files missing? Re-run setup to repair your repository.

submissions.missingParams: Missing organization, classroom, or assignment in the URL.
submissions.collectionNote: Submissions are collected automatically, but new ones can take up to 24 hours to appear.

errors.gradebookLoadHint: The counts below may be incomplete — retry before acting on them.

collect.label: Collect now
collect.title: Collect submissions now
collect.titleEmptyRoster: Add students to the roster before collecting
collect.titleRegrading: Wait for the regrade to finish before collecting
collect.statusRunning: Collection in progress. This page refreshes automatically when it finishes.
collect.statusCompleted: Collection finished. Submissions below are up to date.
collect.statusFailed: The collection run did not complete successfully.
collect.statusFailedHint: You can check or trigger it manually on GitHub.
collect.statusTimeout: Still running after a while. Check its progress on GitHub, or refresh this page once it finishes.

regradeAll.label: Regrade all
regradeAll.title: Re-run the autograder on every submitted repo (submission times don’t change)
regradeAll.titleEmptyRoster: Add students to the roster before regrading
regradeAll.titleCollecting: Wait for collection to finish before regrading
regradeAll.titleRegrading: A regrade is already in progress
regradeAll.statusRunning: Regrade in progress. Re-running the autograder for each submitted repo; collection is paused until it finishes.
regradeAll.statusCompleted_prefix: Regrade started — each repo is re-grading in the background. Click
regradeAll.statusCompleted_suffix: in a few minutes to pull the new scores.
regradeAll.statusFailed: The regrade run did not start successfully.
regradeAll.statusFailedHint: Check the workflow on GitHub, then try again.
regradeAll.statusTimeout: The regrade is taking a while to register. Check its progress on GitHub.
regradeAll.confirmBody1: This re-runs the autograder on every submitted repo's latest commit — useful after fixing a test or updating the autograder. Submission times don't change.
regradeAll.confirmBody2_prefix: Grading runs in the background and can take several minutes; use
regradeAll.confirmBody2_suffix: afterward to pull the new scores.

accept.heading: How students accept
accept.subheading: Share an invite link or CLI command so students can accept this assignment.
accept.unlistedNote: This classroom uses an unlisted URL, so the link includes the access key — treat it like a shared password and send the full link as-is.

stats.showAcceptedNotSubmitted: Show students who accepted but haven't submitted

table.emptyState: No submissions match the current filters.
table.lateRowTitle: The latest submission was pushed after the deadline.
table.gradedAtTitle: When the autograder last (re-)graded this submission. Regrading updates this without changing the submission time.

reviewModal.errorTitle: Couldn't check for a feedback PR
reviewModal.emptyBody_prefix: No Feedback PR has been opened for
reviewModal.emptyBody_suffix: yet. It's created by the assignment's autograde workflow after a graded submission — if the student hasn't submitted (or the assignment has the Feedback PR disabled), there's nothing to review yet.

rowRegrade.title: Regrade this submission
rowRegrade.titleCompleted: Regrade started — grading runs in the background; collect to see new scores
rowRegrade.titleFailed: Regrade failed to start — try again
rowRegrade.confirmBody1_suffix: 's latest commit for this assignment. Their submission time doesn't change.
rowRegrade.confirmBody2_prefix: Grading runs in the background and can take a few minutes; use
rowRegrade.confirmBody2_suffix: afterward to pull the new score.

student.notAccepted_prefix: You haven't accepted this assignment yet.
student.notAccepted_suffix: to get your repository, then push your work to be graded.
student.noGradedYet: No graded submission yet. Push a commit to your assignment repository and the autograder will publish your result here.
student.releasesIntro: Each submission opens its graded release on GitHub, with your score and per-test results.

orgMembers.heading: Members
orgMembers.subtitleSuffix: GitHub organization and the classrooms they belong to.
orgMembers.discrepancy_one: {{count}} student is on a roster but not an organization member.
orgMembers.discrepancy_other: {{count}} students are on a roster but not an organization member.
orgMembers.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, username, or email
orgMembers.selfNotice: This is your signed-in account, so it can't be removed from the organization here.
orgMembers.notMemberPrefix: {{label}} is on a classroom roster but is
orgMembers.notMemberSuffix: . Invite them to restore their access. The invite is sent to their GitHub account by id, so it works even if their username changed.
orgMembers.notMemberNoId: This student is on a roster but is not an organization member. They have no GitHub id on file yet, so invite them from their classroom's Students page.
orgMembers.confirmUnenrollSuffix: organization. Their assignment repositories are not deleted.
orgMembers.removedWithUnenroll_one: {{label}} was removed from the {{org}} organization and unenrolled from {{count}} classroom.
orgMembers.removedWithUnenroll_other: {{label}} was removed from the {{org}} organization and unenrolled from {{count}} classrooms.
orgMembers.warnArchived: {{who}} is still on the archived classroom "{{classroom}}"; unarchive it to remove them from that roster.
orgMembers.warnUnenrollFailed: Couldn't unenroll {{who}} from "{{classroom}}" ({{reason}}); removed the others.
orgMembers.warnOnboardingRepo: Couldn't delete the leftover onboarding repository "{{repo}}" ({{reason}}); you can remove it manually.

emptyRoster.hasRows_suffix: organization yet. An assignment's accept link only works for students who are organization members, so invited students must accept their invite first.
emptyRoster.empty_prefix: This classroom has no students yet. An assignment's accept link only works for students who are members of the
emptyRoster.empty_suffix: organization, so add and enroll students before sharing it.

confirmModal.dangerousPrompt: Are you sure you want to continue? This action may be difficult or impossible to undo.

groupCollaborators.title: Group collaborators
groupCollaborators.onlyOwner_suffix: can manage collaborators for this repository.
groupCollaborators.couldntAdd: Couldn't add — check the username and org membership.
groupCollaborators.tooMany: This assignment has a maximum group size of {{max}} (including the owner).
groupCollaborators.groupFull: Group is full — remove someone to add another collaborator.

failure.rateLimited: GitHub rate limit hit — wait a moment and try again.
failure.forbidden: You don't have permission to change collaborators on this repository.
failure.notFound: Username not found, or not a member of the GitHub organization.
failure.conflict: Already a collaborator, or the request was rejected by GitHub.

error.addAndRemove: Some collaborators could not be added or removed.{{removedNote}} Check the highlighted usernames and Save again to retry.{{suffix}}
error.add: Some collaborators could not be added.{{removedNote}} Check the highlighted usernames and Save again to retry.{{suffix}}
error.remove: Some collaborators could not be removed.{{removedNote}} Check the highlighted usernames and Save again to retry.{{suffix}}

slug.checkError: Couldn’t check existing slugs — you can still try; we’ll re-check on save.
slug.taken: “{{slug}}” is already used in {{classroom}} — pick another.

reuseAssignment.title: Reuse assignment
reuseAssignment.description_suffix: into another classroom in {{org}}. Name, template, tests, runtime, due date, and other settings are copied; student repositories and scores are not.
reuseAssignment.noTargets: There are no other classrooms in {{org}} to reuse this assignment into. Create another classroom first.
reuseAssignment.uniqueHint: Must be unique within the target classroom.

reuseFromClassroom.title: Reuse an existing assignment
reuseFromClassroom.description_prefix: Copy an assignment from another classroom in {{org}} into
reuseFromClassroom.description_suffix: . Name, template, tests, runtime, due date, and other settings are copied; student repositories and scores are not.
reuseFromClassroom.noSources: There are no other classrooms in {{org}} to reuse an assignment from.
reuseFromClassroom.uniqueHint: Must be unique within this classroom.
